vBRIEFInfo.author: panopticon-cli/0.0.0
vBRIEFInfo.description: Plan for PAN-1256: Swarm: deacon orphan recovery races work-agent spawn; no startup grace window for status:starting

plan.title: Swarm: deacon orphan recovery races work-agent spawn; no startup grace window for status:starting
plan.author: agent:claude-opus-4-7

narratives.Problem: PAN-1256 was reopened on 2026-05-24 because the shipped fix (120s WORK_LAUNCHER_GRACE_MS gate in recoverOrphanedAgentsOnce) lacks the unit-test coverage called out in the original acceptance criteria. Without tests there is no guard against regression — a future refactor of the orphan-recovery loop could silently re-introduce the wave-0 race that aborts swarm dispatches.
narratives.Proposal: Add a single new test file `src/lib/cloister/__tests__/deacon-orphan-grace.test.ts` that exercises `recoverOrphanedAgents` against four mocked agent-state scenarios: (1) starting + recent startedAt → not stopped, (2) starting + expired startedAt → stopped, (3) running + no tmux → still stopped (regression for non-starting agents), (4) reviewSubRole + recent startedAt + no launcher pid → not stopped (reviewer 90s grace preserved). The test file uses fake timers to control Date.now() arithmetic and mirrors the mock scaffolding from pan-871-auto-resume.test.ts. The PAN-1122 swarm-smoke acceptance criterion is satisfied by test (1), which deterministically recreates the original failing scenario (29s into spawn). NO production code changes.

autoDecisions.summary: All four tests land in one new file rather than splitting across files or extending an existing file.
autoDecisions.rationale: The tests share mock scaffolding (~80 lines of vi.mock setup), exercise one focused function, and are mechanically identical to author. Splitting would duplicate the scaffolding without aiding reviewability.

autoDecisions.summary: The PAN-1122 swarm smoke-test AC is satisfied by a deterministic unit test that recreates the t=29s race, rather than by an end-to-end swarm run.
autoDecisions.rationale: Running an actual swarm against a benchmark issue is non-deterministic, depends on harness availability, and would not catch a regression any more reliably than a focused unit test that reproduces the exact failure timing. The unit test pinpoints the race condition the smoke test was meant to detect.

autoDecisions.summary: Use literal time offsets (30_000 inside grace, 200_000 outside) rather than importing the constant.
autoDecisions.rationale: WORK_LAUNCHER_GRACE_MS is a function-local const that cannot be imported without refactoring. Literal offsets that are clearly inside and clearly outside the 120s window keep tests robust to future tuning of the constant.

autoDecisions.summary: No production code changes in this issue.
autoDecisions.rationale: The fix is already on main (deacon.ts:5073-5088). The reopener explicitly scoped remaining work to tests/smoke evidence and 'keep reviewer grace behavior unchanged'. Touching the production code would risk re-opening a settled fix.

items.title: Add unit tests for WORK_LAUNCHER_GRACE_MS grace window in recoverOrphanedAgents

narrative.Action: Create `src/lib/cloister/__tests__/deacon-orphan-grace.test.ts` covering the WORK_LAUNCHER_GRACE_MS startup grace window in `recoverOrphanedAgents`. Mirror the mock scaffolding from `pan-871-auto-resume.test.ts` (fs mock, agents.js Effect mocks, tmux.js sessionExistsSync mock returning false, paths.js mock pointing AGENTS_DIR at a fake path). Use `vi.useFakeTimers()` + `vi.setSystemTime()` to control `Date.now()` against each agent's `startedAt`. Write four test cases — three from the issue ACs plus one reviewer-pattern regression. Run `npm test -- deacon-orphan-grace` and ensure all four pass. Then run the full test suite (`npm test`) to confirm no regressions. NO production code changes. Verify by reading `src/lib/cloister/deacon.ts:5073-5088` that the WORK_LAUNCHER_GRACE_MS branch is untouched after your work.

subItems.title: New test file exists at `src/lib/cloister/__tests__/deacon-orphan-grace.test.ts` and uses `vi.useFakeTimers()` in beforeEach / `vi.useRealTimers()` in afterEach

Test: a work agent in `status: 'starting'` with `startedAt` 30s in the past (inside the 120s grace) is NOT marked stopped — assert `recoverOrphanedAgents()` returns `[]` AND `saveAgentState` is never called with `status: 'stopped'` for that agent

Test: a work agent in `status: 'starting'` with `startedAt` 200s in the past (outside the 120s grace) IS marked stopped — assert returned actions array contains the agent dir AND `saveAgentState` is called with `status: 'stopped'`

Test: a work agent in `status: 'running'` with no tmux session IS still marked stopped (no regression for the existing confirmed-dead path — `sessionExistsSync` returns false, no grace branch entered because status is 'running' not 'starting')

Test: a reviewSubRole agent in `status: 'starting'` with `startedAt` 30s in the past and NO `reviewer-launcher.pid` file is NOT marked stopped (preserves the existing 90s REVIEWER_LAUNCHER_GRACE_MS branch — `existsSync` returns false for the pid file, falls into grace check, grace holds)

PAN-1122 swarm-race regression: a swarm slot in `status: 'starting'` with `startedAt` 29s in the past (the exact failure timing from the original 2026-05-21 reproduction) is NOT marked stopped. This is the deterministic stand-in for the PAN-1122 swarm smoke evidence — it reproduces the original failing scenario and proves the fix holds.
All four+ new tests pass under `npm test -- deacon-orphan-grace`
Full test suite passes: `npm test` shows no new failures introduced by the new file
Quality gates pass: `npm run typecheck` and `npm run lint` are clean

Production code at `src/lib/cloister/deacon.ts:5073-5088` (the WORK_LAUNCHER_GRACE_MS branch) is unchanged by this issue — verify with `git diff main -- src/lib/cloister/deacon.ts` showing no edits in that region
